# Evidence Brief - Water & sanitation (SDG 6): Kenya

*Generated by CEDAR on 2026-06-20T14:52:41Z | Glass-box mode: every figure is cited below.*

## Key findings

- [+] Basic drinking-water coverage rose from 53.9 to 64.8 % of population between 2010 and 2023 (+20.2%, +1.4%/yr). [1]
- [!] As of 2023, the value of 64.8 does not yet meet the benchmark of 100 (SDG 6.1: universal drinking water (basic+)); a gap of 35.2 % of population remains. [2]
- [+] Basic sanitation coverage rose from 30.8 to 40.2 % of population between 2010 and 2023 (+30.5%, +2.1%/yr). [3]
- [!] As of 2023, the value of 40.2 does not yet meet the benchmark of 100 (SDG 6.2: universal sanitation (basic+)); a gap of 59.8 % of population remains. [4]

## Data confidence & caveats

- **Basic drinking-water coverage** - confidence: **High** (coverage 100%, latest 2023).
- **Basic sanitation coverage** - confidence: **High** (coverage 100%, latest 2023).

## Methods & reproducibility

All figures are pulled from authoritative APIs and computed deterministically (trend = first vs latest observation; CAGR = compound annual rate; gap = distance to published benchmark). No figure is model-generated. Re-run any number with the query URLs in the evidence ledger below.
